factors.name: US Jobs Data (NFP)
factors.summary: Weaker-than-expected June NFP report released 2026-07-02 reduced Fed rate-hike odds significantly. Multiple sources (Bloomberg, FXStreet) confirm weak US labor data is primary driver of gold rally and lower USD.

factors.name: Fed Rate-Hike Expectations
factors.summary: UBS and market consensus now see Fed 'on hold' with markets overpricing tightening (Investinglive 2026-07-03). Warsh downplaying inflation risk reinforces dovish narrative, lowering real yield expectations.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: Soft jobs data and dovish Fed repricing should compress real yields lower. No nominal yield data provided, but softer inflation-wage disconnect supports real yield compression—primary gold structural support.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: USD weakening broadly post-NFP: GBP/USD near 1.3350, AUD/USD spiking toward 0.6950, NZD/USD above 0.5700, MXN rallying (USD/MXN at 17.48). Weaker USD is structurally bullish for gold.

factors.name: BoJ Verbal Intervention & JPY
factors.summary: Japan Finance Minister Katayama signaled readiness to intervene on FX moves; USD/JPY reversing lower from 161.50 region suggests BoJ jawboning working. Potential yen intervention caps USD strength, supporting gold.

factors.name: China Economic Data
factors.summary: China Services PMI eased modestly to 54.1 vs 54.4 (resilient), export orders hit 20-month high. Mixed signal—domestic demand holding but not accelerating; PBOC set stronger Yuan fixing (6.8047), which supports Asian risk appetite but neutral for gold direction.

factors.name: Geopolitical (Middle East)
factors.summary: US-Iran peace talks progressing; Saudi Hormuz shipments resuming post-war, oil backlog clearing. De-escalation narrative reduces safe-haven demand but eases inflation fears from supply disruptions—offsetting effects.

factors.name: Oil Prices (WTI)
factors.summary: WTI below $68.50 amid easing Middle East tensions and normalized Hormuz flows. Lower oil is disinflationary (supportive of gold via lower real yield pressure) but reduces geopolitical risk premium.

factors.name: ECB Policy
factors.summary: Lagarde defends June rate hike citing persistent core inflation and supply shock (2026-07-02). ECB hawkish stance creates some USD bid vs EUR, but impact muted by stronger Fed dovish shift.

factors.name: Price Action Momentum
factors.summary: Gold +1.35% in 4h and +2.99% in 24h post-NFP, now approaching $4,200 per Bloomberg headline. Move is already underway but not yet extremely extended; momentum remains positive.

screening.narrative: Gold is rallying on a combination of softer-than-expected US June NFP data (released 2026-07-02) and Fed dovish repricing, which compresses real yield expectations and weakens the broad USD. Markets are now pricing in sustained Fed hold/pause vs. prior tightening bets. BoJ verbal intervention is capping USD/JPY downside, reducing carry unwind risk. Geopolitical de-escalation in the Middle East (Saudi shipments resuming, US-Iran talks holding) reduces inflation tail risk but also diminishes safe-haven demand. Valuations near $4,200 have attracted some technical interest, but the move is only ~6 hours into Phase 2—not yet extended. Upcoming US market closure (July 4) on Friday will reduce liquidity and may trap fast money.
catalysts_ahead: US market closure Friday July 4 (low liquidity, potential squeeze risk)
Next CPI/PCE print (date not specified in news, assume mid-July)
FOMC meeting (date not specified, assume mid-July forward guidance critical)
Powell/Waller/Williams speeches (none imminent per news window)
China economic data calendar (PMI next cycle)
BoJ meeting/Governor commentary (BoJ hawkish on services inflation per 2026-07-03 report)
screening.risk_warning: Gold move is already +3% in 24h and in PHASE_2—extended gains increase pullback risk if NFP narrative revises, USD stabilizes on new supports, or real yields rebound on incoming inflation surprises. US market closure Friday will reduce volume and limit new catalyst flow. If upcoming CPI/PCE data surprises hot, or if Fed speakers hint at higher-for-longer, real yields could compress momentum. Conversely, if recession fears escalate (equity selloff), gold could break higher as risk-off trade, but that is not the dominant narrative yet.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in progress and moderately extended post-NFP (Phase 2). Best entry for new longs is a pullback retest of $4,160–$4,175; chasing near $4,200 risks late entry into a corrective bounce. For shorts, wait for a failed break of $4,210 resistance and retest of moving averages before considering tactical fades. Low liquidity Friday increases chop risk—be cautious on size.

factors.name: US Employment Data (NFP)
factors.summary: Weaker-than-expected US NFP data reduced Fed rate hike expectations, lowering real yield pressure. Multiple sources cite soft jobs report weighing on USD and supporting gold rally to near $4,200 (Bloomberg, FXStreet).

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy Outlook
factors.summary: UBS sees markets overpricing Fed tightening; Warsh downplays inflation risk despite hike bets. Trump renewing push to reshape Fed adds uncertainty. Market repricing away from hikes is constructive for gold.

factors.name: US Dollar (DXY)
factors.summary: USD weakening broadly after soft NFP: GBP/USD near 1.3350, AUD/USD above 0.6900, EUR/USD near 1.1420. Weaker dollar is primary driver of gold's 24h rally per multiple FX reports.

factors.name: Real Yields / Nominal Yields
factors.summary: Soft employment data lowers rate hike odds, which should compress real yields (though no explicit TIPS yield cited). UBS sees elevated yields as buying opportunity in bonds, signaling expected decline.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: Mixed signals: Middle East tensions easing (oil steadies, Hormuz flows increasing per Kpler/Bloomberg), but Iran vows 'decisive response' to US interference. Net easing of tensions is marginally bearish for safe-haven bid, but not yet material.

factors.name: Japanese Yen / BoJ Intervention
factors.summary: Japan Finance Minister signals readiness to act on FX moves; USD/JPY reversed from 161.50 amid intervention speculation. Yen strength can support gold via carry-trade unwind, but intervention is verbal so far.

factors.name: Oil Prices
factors.summary: WTI below $68.50, easing on reduced Middle East tensions and increased Hormuz flows. Lower oil is disinflationary but not a strong direct gold driver at current levels.

factors.name: China Economic Data
factors.summary: China services PMI eased slightly to 54.1 vs 54.4, but export orders hit 20-month high. Vietnam GDP beat at 8.39%. Supports risk sentiment but not a primary gold driver; PBOC gold buying not mentioned in recent window.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows
factors.summary: No recent data on GLD/IAU flows in the news window. Cannot assess institutional positioning changes.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: Equities stabilized after two days of tech-led losses; stocks 'stabilize' per Bloomberg. Not a strong risk-off or risk-on signal; gold moving on real yield/USD dynamics rather than panic bid.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No fresh data on PBoC, RBI, or other central bank purchases in the 24h window. Structural bid remains but not a new catalyst.

factors.name: ECB Policy
factors.summary: Lagarde defends June rate hike despite softer Eurozone inflation, prioritizing underlying inflation persistence. ECB hawkishness supports EUR but is marginal for gold vs. Fed dynamics.

confirmation.narrative: Gold extended its rally toward $4,200 after weaker-than-expected US NFP data triggered broad USD weakness and reduced Fed rate-hike expectations. UBS argues markets are overpricing Fed tightening, and Warsh's dovish lean adds to the narrative that real yields have peaked. The move is Phase 2—price already up +2.99% in 24h—so the initial impulse has been captured. Geopolitical tailwinds from Middle East tensions are fading as Hormuz flows normalize, removing one safe-haven support, but the USD/real-yield story remains dominant.
catalysts_ahead: US markets closed Friday (July 4 Independence Day) - thin liquidity
Any fresh Fed communication from Warsh or other officials
Japan intervention risk (verbal warnings escalating)
China inflation data (CPI/PPI) typically early-to-mid month
Next major US catalyst: CPI release (likely ~July 10-12)
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Fed officials push back hard on dovish repricing, signaling hikes still on table; (2) USD reverses sharply higher on renewed risk-off or hawkish surprise; (3) gold fails to hold $4,125 support, signaling profit-taking after extended rally. Holiday-thinned liquidity Friday increases risk of whipsaw moves.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move already extended +3% in 24h; wait for pullback toward $4,125-4,150 support zone before adding longs, or use tight stops if chasing current levels into holiday weekend.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: Weak US June NFP (+57K vs +110K expected) triggered sharp repricing of Fed rate-hike odds downward. Lower real yields are the single most correlated bullish driver for gold; this is now flowing through markets.

factors.name: Fed Policy Pivot
factors.summary: Markets are repricing out Fed tightening bets following soft labor data. UBS and other analysts now see Fed likely on hold; this dovish repricing is directly supportive of gold. Warsh downplaying inflation risk adds weight.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY trades near 100.80 and under pressure; weak NFP sent broad USD selling across majors (GBP, EUR, AUD, NZD, CAD, INR all firmer). Weaker USD is structurally bullish for gold.

factors.name: Nominal US 10y Yield
factors.summary: US equity selloff in tech (Nasdaq -0.8%) and mixed rotation suggest yields likely extending lower; lower nominal rates support gold. Holiday-thinned US markets Friday may amplify moves.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations / Recent Data
factors.summary: No new CPI/PCE print in news window; prior focus is on cooling labor market. No fresh inflation surprises to contradict the dovish repricing.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: Iran-Hormuz tensions persist but no acute escalation this window; geopolitical premium remains modest. Oil easing toward $68 suggests some risk-off normalization rather than panic.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying / ETF Flows
factors.summary: No specific CB buying or ETF flow data in news. Structural bid from PBoC/RBI remains backdrop but not a fresh catalyst today.

factors.name: Price Action Momentum
factors.summary: Gold up +2.83% in 24h and +1.19% in 4h, building on 3-day rally to near 2-week highs (~$4,200 area per Bloomberg headline). Third day of gains following NFP release.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment / Equities
factors.summary: US equity markets closed mixed (Dow +1.1%, Nasdaq -0.8%); Asian equities clawed back losses (Taiwan +1.94%, Korea +5% after -10% prior two days). No panic, but tech rotation underway — mild risk-off tone supports gold.

factors.name: AI Trade / Tech Chop
factors.summary: Invesco analysts note AI rally stretched in parts; pricing power weakening per Bloomberg. South Korea and other tech hubs saw heavy volatility. This uncertainty may support safe-haven gold but not a dominant driver today.

screening.narrative: Gold has rallied for three consecutive days on the back of a significant dovish repricing of Fed policy following weak US June NFP (+57K vs +110K expected), which slashed rate-hike odds and sent real yields lower. The USD Index has weakened materially across all major pairs, a structural tailwind for gold. Nominal US 10y yields are likely extending lower as markets rotate away from rate-hike bets, with equities showing a tech-to-value rotation (Nasdaq weakness). Although the move is already underway and extended (+2.83% in 24h), momentum remains positive and no major contradicting catalysts have emerged—this is classic Phase 2 move (in progress, testing resistance).
catalysts_ahead: US markets closed Friday for Independence Day (holiday trading, thin liquidity — may amplify moves or create gaps)
Eurozone/UK final PMIs due Friday European session (consensus data, low market impact per news)
BoJ Katayama verbal intervention warning on yen (currency risk, potential for USD/JPY vol)
No CPI/PCE/NFP data due in next 72h; next major release likely July 8–9 area
Iran funeral events July 3–9 (geopolitical monitoring, Hormuz risk low-grade ongoing)
screening.risk_warning: Move is already +2.83% in 24h and building into resistance at $4,200–$4,220. If gold fails to break and hold above $4,200 and closes the week below, a near-term pullback toward $4,150–$4,170 support is likely. Sharp USD rally on unexpected hawkish surprise or CPI beat would reverse the bias quickly. Thin holiday liquidity Friday could also trigger sharp retracements. Confidence is capped at 7 because move is in Phase 2 (extended) and next major data catalyst (CPI/PCE) is >5 days away, leaving room for consolidation or pullback into Monday reopen.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended; suggest waiting for pullback to $4,150–$4,170 support or a retest of $4,200 resistance before fresh BUY. If already long from earlier rally, consider taking partial profit at resistance and holding remainder for break above $4,220 on volume.

factors.name: US Labor Market / Fed Rate Hike Bets
factors.summary: US June NFP came in at +57K vs +110K expected (per Investinglive), triggering dovish repricing and reducing Fed rate hike odds. UBS now sees Fed on hold despite elevated yields, calling current market pricing of hikes as overblown. This is strongly bullish for gold as it lowers real yield expectations.

factors.name: US Dollar (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY faces pressure near 100.80 following weak NFP data, with multiple FX sources citing broad USD weakness. EUR/USD climbing toward 1.1450-1.1470, GBP/USD near 1.3350-1.3360, all reflecting dollar selling. Weaker USD is directly bullish for gold.

factors.name: Real Yields (Implied)
factors.summary: With Fed hike bets receding and nominal yields likely capped (per UBS commentary on elevated yields as buying opportunity in bonds), real yields face downward pressure. FXStreet explicitly cites 'receding Fed hike bets' as driver of gold rally toward two-week top.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium
factors.summary: Iran warns of 'decisive response' to any US miscalculation in Strait of Hormuz (FXStreet), with seven-day funeral for Ayatollah Khamenei beginning and European powers accepting some Hormuz fees will be inevitable (Bloomberg). Russia launched 'most massive' attack on Kyiv killing 30+ (BBC). Safe-haven bid intact.

factors.name: Oil Prices
factors.summary: WTI trading below $68.50, down modestly as Middle East tensions ease (per FXStreet and Investinglive). Citi forecasts oil may slump to $60 by year-end as Hormuz shock fades (Bloomberg). Lower oil reduces inflation tail risk, which is mixed for gold but removes one bullish input.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment / AI Trade Jitters
factors.summary: Mixed equity action with Dow +1.1% but Nasdaq -0.8% on rotation into value stocks (Investinglive). AI trade concerns mounting with Bloomberg citing 'AI Trade Losing Key Signals' and pricing per unit drifting lower. South Korean stocks volatile (+5% rebound after -10% prior two sessions). Gold acting as safe-haven rather than risk-on asset currently.

factors.name: Central Bank Policy (BoJ)
factors.summary: Japan FinMin Katayama repeated verbal intervention warnings, ready to act on FX moves with USD/JPY near 161.50. Options traders paying up for yen hedges ahead of thin US holiday trading (Bloomberg). Yen intervention risk is event risk but not directional driver for gold currently.

factors.name: Physical Demand / Seasonality
factors.summary: No specific news on Indian/Chinese physical demand in this window. China services PMI came in slightly softer at 54.1 vs 54.4 prior (RatingDog), suggesting stable but not surging domestic activity. Structural demand steady but not a catalyst in last 24h.

factors.name: Gold Price Action / Technical
factors.summary: Gold has rallied +2.83% in 24h and +1.19% in 4h, trading near two-week highs around $4,188-4,200 (Bloomberg, FXStreet). Move is three days in a row of gains, suggesting momentum but also extension. FXStreet notes gold 'clings to gains' and 'builds on recovery,' indicating market is consolidating gains rather than surging further.

confirmation.narrative: Gold's three-day rally to $4,188 is driven by weaker-than-expected US NFP data (57K vs 110K) that has dramatically reduced Fed rate hike expectations and pressured the dollar. Real yields are facing downward pressure as UBS and others now see the Fed on hold despite elevated nominal yields. Geopolitical tensions remain supportive with Iran-US Hormuz standoff escalating and Russia intensifying Ukraine attacks. However, the move is now extended (+2.83% in 24h), placing us in Phase 3 where chasing is risky ahead of the US holiday weekend with thin liquidity.
catalysts_ahead: US markets closed July 4 (Friday) - thin liquidity risk
Final Eurozone PMIs (today, limited impact expected per Investinglive)
Japan potential FX intervention (officials on alert, timing unknown)
Iran-US Hormuz negotiations ongoing (Qatari mediators involved)
Next major US data: likely ISM Services and JOLTS next week
confirmation.risk_warning: Move is extended after three-day rally. If dollar stabilizes or geopolitical tensions ease further (Iran-US deal progress), gold could retrace 50-70% of recent gains back toward $4,100-4,120. US markets closed Friday creates thin liquidity and potential for whipsaw. Any hawkish Fed speak from Warsh (who per UBS is downplaying inflation risk) or Williams would challenge the dovish repricing.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move is late-stage (Phase 3) after +2.83% rally; wait for pullback toward $4,150-4,160 support or consolidation above $4,180 before adding longs. Avoid chasing into thin holiday liquidity.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: Softer US NFP yesterday (+57K vs +110K expected) triggered dovish Fed repricing; July hike odds dropped to 17% from 30%, reducing near-term rate hike expectations and depressing real yields. Lower real yields are the single strongest structural bid for gold.

factors.name: USD Index & DXY
factors.summary: US Dollar has weakened across the board post-NFP; EUR/USD rallied to near 1.1455, GBP/USD strong at 1.3385 (strongest week in 3 months), and USD/JPY fell sharply to two-week lows near 161.00. Weaker DXY is bullish for gold.

factors.name: Fed Policy Repricing
factors.summary: Market now pricing only ~30 bps of total Fed hikes by December 2026 (down from previous expectations). Dovish shift removes headwind to gold and supports lower real yields regime.

factors.name: BoJ & JPY Dynamics
factors.summary: Suspected Japanese FX intervention support yen amid weaker USD; Japan's wage growth confirmed at 5.01% for third year, reinforcing BoJ hiking bias. Carry-trade unwind and USD/JPY weakness benefit risk-off, gold-supportive environment.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations
factors.summary: No fresh CPI/PCE data in this window. UK services PMI weak at 48.8 (near 3.5-year low for activity), Eurozone composite at 50.0 (stagnation), and France composite at 47.2—all suggest disinflationary backdrop. ECB Lagarde mulling early exit signals dovish shift.

factors.name: Nominal 10Y Treasury Yield
factors.summary: Dovish repricing and weaker economic data (UK, Eurozone services contraction) should support lower nominal yields; this is bullish for gold in real yield terms.

factors.name: Oil Prices
factors.summary: WTI hovering mid-$68s after bouncing from late-Feb lows; US-Iran talks progressing (LNG traffic resuming via Hormuz); no acute supply shock. Oil weakness may reinforce disinflationary narrative, mildly supportive of gold.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Safe-Haven Flows
factors.summary: US stock outflows accelerate (largest in 3+ months per BofA), Dow +1.1% but Nasdaq -0.8%; equity rotation into value suggests modest flight-to-safety. Mixed sentiment does not strongly favor gold as safe-haven vs. competing flows to fixed income.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risks
factors.summary: Iran funeral/mourning underway, but no new acute escalation. Strait of Hormuz fee talks ongoing but ceasefire narrative (US-Iran) prevails. Ukraine tensions persist but not dominating headlines today. Geopolitics remain ambient, not acute trigger.

factors.name: Price Action & Momentum
factors.summary: Gold +2.83% in 24h, +1.19% in 4h; on track for first weekly gain in a month per TD Securities (expects push toward $4,280/oz near-term, support $3,900/oz). Momentum is clearly bullish, but move is established, not fresh.

screening.narrative: Gold is rallying sharply on the back of dovish Fed repricing triggered by softer US June NFP (+57K vs +110K expected). Real yields are compressing as markets price out twin Fed hikes, supporting the yellow metal. Broad USD weakness (EUR/USD, GBP/USD strength, USD/JPY falls) and suspected BoJ intervention add tailwinds. Eurozone and UK economic data confirm disinflationary slowdown, reinforcing the case for lower near-term rates. The move is 3 days old and +2.83% in 24h, placing us in mid-momentum phase rather than early-cycle breakout.
catalysts_ahead: US FOMC Minutes (date pending, likely next week) — could clarify whether two hikes remain or shift more dovish
Fed speakers (Powell, Williams, Waller) — any guidance on rate path could re-anchor expectations
US CPI/PCE release (mid-month window) — inflation print will be critical to real yield narrative
Next NFP (early month) — labor data surprise again could accelerate or reverse recent dovish repricing
ECB Lagarde commentary — early exit talk could signal Euro weakness, supporting gold via DXY
US Independence Day (July 4) — markets closed today, Monday will see holiday backlog; reduced liquidity risk
screening.risk_warning: Primary invalidation: if upcoming inflation data surprises hot (CPI/PCE beats) or Fed speakers signal hawkish hold, real yields could re-steepen and kill the rally. Secondary: if equity sentiment suddenly stabilizes and risk-on re-ignites, gold could face profit-taking and revert to $4,100 support. Tertiary: if USD intervention by BoJ stabilizes JPY and reverses carry unwind, DXY could re-find support, capping gold gains.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE 2 (established rally, +2.83% in 24h). Avoid chasing into resistance; consider entry on pullback to $4,130–$4,150 zone or await retest of 4h MA. If breaks $4,250+, reassess for PHASE_3 late-entry signal.

factors.name: US NFP & Fed Rate Expectations
factors.summary: US June NFP came in at +57K vs +110K expected (Investinglive). This weak data triggered dovish repricing: July Fed hike odds dropped from 30% to 17%, September now ~50%. Lower rate hike expectations reduce real yields and are bullish for gold.

factors.name: US Dollar Weakness
factors.summary: USD weakened broadly post-NFP (Investinglive). DXY under pressure across the board. ING notes EUR/USD action shows limited EUR strength, meaning USD weakness is the primary driver—directly bullish for gold.

factors.name: Real Yields (implied)
factors.summary: Though not explicitly cited, softer NFP + reduced Fed hike odds imply lower real yields ahead. TD Securities notes gold driven above $4,100/oz by 'weaker US jobs data and reduced odds of a 2026 Fed rate hike'—classic real yield decline setup.

factors.name: Technical & Positioning
factors.summary: TD Securities expects gold trending toward $4,280/oz near term with support at $3,900/oz. FXStreet reports gold 'on track to snap four-week losing streak' and 'retains bullish bias for third straight day' near 1.5-week high.

factors.name: Geopolitical (Middle East/Iran)
factors.summary: Iran's slain leader funeral events underway, US-Iran talks in focus, Strait of Hormuz shipping resuming (Qatar LNG traffic). Tensions not escalating further but not resolving either—safe-haven bid not adding nor subtracting meaningfully today.

factors.name: Oil Prices
factors.summary: WTI hovering mid-$68s, Citi forecasts oil may slump to $60 by year-end as Hormuz shock fades. Lower oil = lower inflation expectations, mildly supportive for dovish Fed stance but not a primary gold driver today.

factors.name: Other Central Banks (BoJ/ECB)
factors.summary: Japan wage hikes confirmed at 5.01% for third year (bullish JPY/BoJ tightening, which can weaken USD). Suspected FX intervention yesterday supported yen. ECB's Lagarde mulls early exit, Eurozone PMI revised to stagnation. Mixed signals, not decisive for gold.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: BofA reports largest US stock outflows in 3+ months. Rotation into value (Dow +1.1%, Nasdaq -0.8% yesterday). Mixed equity action—gold acting as safe-haven and benefiting from lower USD/yields, not pure risk-on rally.

factors.name: Silver & Precious Metals Complex
factors.summary: Silver rose 2.72% to $62.60 (FXStreet), indicating broad precious metals strength alongside gold—confirms bullish momentum is genuine, not just gold-specific.

factors.name: Move Already Extended
factors.summary: Gold is +1.19% in 4h, +2.83% in 24h, and up for third straight day to 1.5-week high. This is a Phase 3 move—most of the short-term upside from the NFP catalyst has already been captured. Entry here is late and risky.

confirmation.narrative: Gold's rally is driven by softer US NFP (+57K vs +110K expected) triggering a dovish repricing of Fed expectations and broad USD weakness—the two most powerful bullish factors for gold. However, the move is now in its third day (+2.83% in 24h) and approaching TD Securities' near-term target of $4,280. While the fundamental bias remains constructive (lower real yields, weaker dollar), the tactical risk is high: this is a Phase 3 extended move where most short-term upside has been realized. Markets are also heading into a US holiday weekend with thin liquidity.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC June meeting minutes release (watch for clarity on divided Committee and conditions for further hikes)
Next major US data: CPI or PCE (timing not specified in news but critical for validating dovish repricing)
BoJ policy meeting watch (Japan wage data reinforces tightening case, could impact USD/JPY and indirectly gold)
Geopolitical: Iran funeral events through next week, ongoing Hormuz shipping fee negotiations
confirmation.risk_warning: Move is extended after 3-day rally; chasing here risks buying the top of short-term range. A hawkish FOMC minutes release or stronger-than-expected US data (CPI, retail sales) could quickly reverse dovish Fed repricing, strengthening USD and pressuring gold back toward $4,100 or lower. Thin holiday liquidity increases volatility risk. Wait for pullback toward $4,100-$4,050 for better risk/reward.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move extended—wait for pullback to $4,100-$4,050 support zone before considering fresh long entries. Chasing at current levels offers poor risk/reward into holiday weekend.
